svg.text: Ribbon Geometry, Texture Layout & Gap Architecture
Double Reflection method (Wang et al. 2008) — pre-computed for entire path
(0 for strand A, π for B)
9 mesh segments with 5 tiles (wrap: segments 5–8 reuse tiles 0–3)
Segment N holds two texture references (current + next) and blends per flowOffset:
1. wholeTiles = ⌊flowOffset⌋ 2. tileFlowOffset += wholeTiles (shifts all segment tile pairs) 3. flowOffset -= wholeTiles
→ New material's "current" = old "next" — seamless visual transition, no 1-frame glitch.
Virtual Cycle (tileCount = 5, gapSize = 1): cycleLength = N + G = 6
tile region [0, N) → render fragment normally
// Continuous virtual position for THIS fragment:
float virtualPos = uBasePos + shiftedU; // shiftedU = vUv.x + uFlowOffset
float cyclePos = mod(virtualPos, cycleLen); // position within [0, cycleLen)
if (cyclePos >= uTileCount) discard; // gap region → transparent fragment
// else: render tile as normal (dual-texture flow shader still active)
• Non-integer gap sizes (e.g. 0.5 tiles) cause integer
segment math to skip gaps in some repetitions.
(sub-segment precision) so every cycle gets an
identical gap regardless of integer boundaries.
• Geometry never changes — all effects are shader-only.
5. Data Flow Summary — Gap Slider to Shader Uniforms
persisted in store for UI reactivity
Resolve tile indices via virtual cycle
uBasePos uTileCount uGapSize uFlowOffset uTexArrayCurrent uTexArrayNext uLayer
